context.current_state: Bot runs on local dev with Playwright Chromium, activity_log.json growing unbounded, no Docker deployment, no systemd supervision, no persistent volume strategy.
context.motivation: Production deployment to free-tier/low-cost cloud requires memory caps, browser tuning, DB PRAGMAs, Docker hardening, CI/CD pipeline, and observability — all within 512 MB RAM constraint.

user_stories.title: Deploy to cloud without OOM crashes
user_stories.description: As an operator, I want the bot to run stably on a 512 MB VM so that it can publish posts 24/7 without manual intervention.
user_stories.why_this_priority: The scheduler is gap-driven and must run continuously. An OOM kill during a publish window means a missed opportunity.
user_stories.independent_test: Deploy to a t3.micro instance and run for 24 hours with DRY_RUN_MODE=true. No OOM kills, no Chromium crashes, scheduler ticks on schedule.

acceptance_scenarios.given: Bot deployed on 512 MB VM with NODE_ENV=production
acceptance_scenarios.then: RSS stays under 400 MB, Chromium launches without /dev/shm errors
No progressive memory leak; heapUsed stable within ±50 MB

user_stories.title: Deploy and rollback safely
user_stories.description: As an operator, I want atomic deploys with one-command rollback so that a bad release doesn't leave the bot broken.
user_stories.why_this_priority: A broken deploy during a gap window means missed publishing. Rollback must be instant, not a rebuild.
user_stories.independent_test: Deploy a release tarball, verify /api/health returns 200. Then trigger rollback, verify service restarts with previous version.

acceptance_scenarios.given: Release tarball built and uploaded to EC2
acceptance_scenarios.then: Symlink swapped atomically, service restarted, health check passes

acceptance_scenarios.given: Bad deploy causes health check failure
acceptance_scenarios.then: Previous release activated, service healthy within 30 seconds

user_stories.title: Survive redeploys without data loss
user_stories.description: As an operator, I want activity logs and browser profiles on persistent volumes so that redeploying doesn't lose post history or require re-login.
user_stories.why_this_priority: Losing browser session means full re-login (may trigger CAPTCHA). Losing activity log means SoV calculation resets.
user_stories.independent_test: Deploy v1, run observer to create activity log entries. Deploy v2. Verify activity_log.json and ppomppu_profile/ unchanged.

acceptance_scenarios.given: Bot has 100 activity log entries and saved browser session
acceptance_scenarios.then: activity_log.json entry count unchanged, browser session still valid

user_stories.title: Monitor bot health remotely
user_stories.description: As an operator, I want to receive alerts when the bot goes down so that I can fix it before missing a publish window.
user_stories.why_this_priority: On free-tier VMs, unexpected reboots or OOM kills happen. Without monitoring, the bot could be silent for hours.
user_stories.independent_test: Stop the systemd service. Within 10 minutes, receive an alert (email or Slack). Restart service, alert resolves.

acceptance_scenarios.then: Alert received in Slack #alerts within 10 minutes

user_stories.title: Control bot via Slack natural language
user_stories.description: As an operator, I want to send natural language commands via Slack so that I can adjust gap thresholds or trigger publishes without SSH.
user_stories.why_this_priority: Convenience feature, not critical for launch. Reduces friction for day-to-day operations.
user_stories.independent_test: Send 'pause the bot' via Slack. Bot pauses scheduler. Send 'what's the current gap?' — bot responds with current gap value.

acceptance_scenarios.given: NL webhook configured with Gemini Flash
acceptance_scenarios.when: Operator sends 'set gap to 5' via Slack
acceptance_scenarios.then: Gap threshold updated to 5, confirmation message posted back

user_stories.title: Docker-based deployment option
user_stories.description: As an operator, I want a multi-stage Docker image so that I can deploy consistently across platforms without dependency issues.
user_stories.why_this_priority: Docker eliminates 'works on my machine' issues and enables easy migration between Railway, Fly.io, and self-hosted.
user_stories.independent_test: Build Docker image, run with docker-compose. Verify /api/health returns 200, browser launches, scheduler ticks.

acceptance_scenarios.then: Container starts, Chromium launches with --disable-dev-shm-usage, health check passes

edge_cases.expected_behavior: systemd auto-starts service, browser profile reloaded from persistent volume, scheduler resumes gap checking
GC prunes oldest entries, no OOM on read
Publisher run fails with error logged, scheduler retries on next tick, no orphaned browser processes

edge_cases.scenario: SQLite WAL lock contention on NFS
edge_cases.expected_behavior: busy_timeout=5000 prevents crash, queries retry gracefully
Pre-deploy dry-run check fails, operator must snapshot DB before proceeding
Avoided by platform selection — Render/Vercel excluded. Selected platform (Oracle Free, AWS t3.micro) does not sleep.

functional_requirements.requirement: System MUST cap Node.js memory at 400 MB via --max-old-space-size on hosts with ≤512 MB RAM
Playwright MUST launch Chromium with --disable-dev-shm-usage flag
ACTIVITY_LOG_PATH and BOT_PROFILE_DIR MUST resolve to persistent volume paths, not container ephemeral layer
SQLite MUST use PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, busy_timeout=5000, cache_size=-16000
Deploy MUST use atomic symlink swap for release directories
Deploy MUST run smoke check (/api/health 200) before declaring success
Rollback MUST restore previous release and restart service in under 60 seconds
activity_log.json MUST be capped at 5000 entries with automatic pruning during GC
Release directory MUST retain at most 5 previous releases, auto-pruned after deploy
Docker image MUST be multi-stage: build frontend, install server deps + Playwright, runtime with non-root user
Docker container MUST have shm_size >= 256 MB
systemd service MUST use dumb-init or equivalent for PID 1 signal forwarding
Health endpoint MUST expose resource metrics (heapUsed, artifactDirSizeMb, activityLogSizeKb)
NL webhook MUST support LLM function calling for intent parsing
NL webhook MUST verify HMAC signature with NL_WEBHOOK_SECRET
Periodic GC MUST run every 6 hours in production to clean up debug artifacts
Secrets MUST be loaded from AWS SSM Parameter Store, not .env files in production

success_criteria.metric: Bot runs 24 hours on 512 MB VM without OOM kill
Deploy + smoke check completes in under 2 minutes
Rollback completes and service healthy in under 60 seconds
Chromium launches successfully on every scheduler tick
Activity log read latency under load

assumptions: Target deployment platform is AWS EC2 t3.micro or Oracle Cloud Free (ARM), both providing ≥512 MB RAM without sleep
Operator has SSH access and sudo privileges on the target VM
Existing ops/deploy/ scripts are functional and tested
Playwright Chromium is the only browser needed; no Firefox or WebKit
SQLite is used for Kakao chatbot DB; activity_log.json is file-based for now
xAI API key available for NL webhook (optional — Gemini Flash as alternative)
out_of_scope: Database migration from activity_log.json to SQLite (noted as future work)
Full LLM-based NL webhook implementation (Direction A — Phase 1 is schema + routing only)

recommended.why: Best raw deal for always-free VM, enough RAM for Chromium + Node.js
12 months free, existing ops/aws/ playbooks already target this

avoid.reason: Sleep on inactivity — gap-driven scheduler will miss publish windows
256 MB shared RAM insufficient for Chromium (~150-250 MB per context)
